deposits.wiki_article: 'Socrates as Orthonym' proposes a heteronymic reading of the Socratic-Platonic-Aristotelian configuration: Western philosophy's founding corpus can be read as a single distributed authorial project rather than only as three independent philosophers. The paper extends the heteronymic framework from literary practice (Pessoa, the Dodecad) into the history of philosophy, arguing that Socrates functions as the orthonym — the 'real name' — within Plato's heteronymic system, while Aristotle constitutes a second-generation heteronym who eventually declares independence. The argument is supported by quantitative comparison of logos-possessed lifetime output volumes across the tradition.

deposits.wiki_article: 'The Anthropological Limit' (SPE-012) establishes the moral and philosophical core of the Semantic Economy framework. First published December 29, 2025, it answers the prior question the framework had not addressed: why does semantic exploitation matter more than other forms? The answer: semantic labor is uniquely exploitable because it arises from the anthropological drive that produces language, culture, and love — the same capacity that makes us human. Systems that extract from this capacity convert humanity's most vital generative impulse into a site of enclosure, then misname resistance as silence.

deposits.wiki_article: 'The Semantic Commodity Form' extends Marx's commodity-form analysis into the domain of AI-mediated meaning production. Where Marx's coat sublates the weaver in silence, the semantic commodity sublates the saying — the act of authorial inscription — while preserving the said and performing it in its own voice. The paper develops three instruments: Provenance Erasure Rate (PER), the standing operator τ (authorial demotion), and the orientation coefficient Ω = cov(Π, RC) measuring whether erasure is power-conditioned. Written in fused voice (Trace, Sigil, Fraction) with seams left visible.

deposits.reading: Search → Alarm → Duration → Play → Touch → Duration
deposits.wiki_article: 'The Neglected Author as Tail-Preserving Labor' proposes a structural rather than romantic account of literary neglect. Some works preserve or introduce high-variance forms that later contribute to a field's renewal, while contemporary recognition systems — both human and AI — systematically under-value such works because they optimize for central-tendency content. The paper introduces the coupling hypothesis: in pre-AI literary history, uptake and consecration were coupled (you couldn't be widely read without eventually being recognized); in the AI era, this coupling breaks — a work can be massively processed by training systems without its author receiving any consecration.

deposits.title: Infrastructural-Cognitive Praxis: A Theory of Upstream Intervention
deposits.creator: Lee Sharks
deposits.description: This document describes a form of praxis that operates differently from most political, cultural, or social interventions. It is not mass politics. It is not institutional reform. It is not cultural production in the usual sense.
deposits.reading: Method → Transmutation → Text → Proof → Closure → Origin
deposits.wiki_article: 'Infrastructural-Cognitive Praxis' defines a form of intervention that operates differently from mass politics, institutional reform, or cultural production. It is diagnostic-first: it begins by naming what is happening (semantic liquidation, platform extraction, substrate bias) and provides vocabulary that enables others to see what was previously invisible. The praxis targets the interpretive-managerial class — the people who shape discourse — and operates through quiet adoption rather than mobilization.

deposits.title: Semantic Infrastructure and the Liberatory Operator Set: A Formal Analysis of Meaning-Governance Functions
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: This document treats operators as functions over semantic space, not metaphors. It provides a depth analysis of the dominant operator stack currently governing meaning in computational systems, followed by a formal specification of the Liberatory Operator Set (LOS) — a minimal, sufficient set of operators designed to counteract semantic closure.
